name: Dr. Amy Acton
subject: I worked for a Republican Governor. Now I’m running to be a Democratic Governor.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Serve America PAC
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101501271/526146716/-491556796?refcode=250203_Serve_Acton_DemGovernor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzFiYTViMC04YWUyLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk3ZTkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EdEIQcQ8Ae-xI9m9UTnlSfD-t_nLj6sMqJ2dkbjFx6o=)
---
Friend, I’m Dr. Amy Acton and I’m a Democrat running for Governor of Ohio. I’m a doctor, not a politician. I care deeply about families, public health and advocating for Ohioans who are struggling. **But when Governor DeWine asked me to join his administration as Director of the Ohio Department of Public Health in 2019, I had no idea what was to come.** When COVID-19 spread like wildfire I worked side-by-side with Democrats and Republicans alike to defend Ohioans against the pandemic, the scourge of opioids, and fight for public health across our state. I believe that the best way to find solutions and help people is to work with folks from all walks of life and with all different viewpoints. And that’s exactly how I will lead as Governor. **The problems we face don’t care if you’re a Democrat or a Republican. They just need fixing. And friend, I don’t care if you’re a Democrat or a Republican, either. I’ll fight for you and your family just the same.** But I can’t win this race alone, that’s why I’m asking for your help today because we have set a goal to raise $50,000 to kick off this campaign. [**If you’re ready to put an end to political games and get to work solving problems, please split a donation between Serve America and my campaign before midnight tonight.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101501272/526146717/1804934404?refcode=250203_Serve_Acton_DemGovernor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzFiYTViMC04YWUyLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk3ZTkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EdEIQcQ8Ae-xI9m9UTnlSfD-t_nLj6sMqJ2dkbjFx6o=) [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101501273/526146718/-927293987?refcode=250203_Serve_Acton_DemGovernor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzFiYTViMC04YWUyLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk3ZTkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EdEIQcQ8Ae-xI9m9UTnlSfD-t_nLj6sMqJ2dkbjFx6o=) Be well, Dr. Amy Acton [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101501274/526146719/-927293987?refcode=250203_Serve_Acton_DemGovernor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzFiYTViMC04YWUyLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk3ZTkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EdEIQcQ8Ae-xI9m9UTnlSfD-t_nLj6sMqJ2dkbjFx6o=) [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101501275/526146720/-927293987?refcode=250203_Serve_Acton_DemGovernor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzFiYTViMC04YWUyLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk3ZTkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EdEIQcQ8Ae-xI9m9UTnlSfD-t_nLj6sMqJ2dkbjFx6o=)

name: Mark Warner
subject: Higher prices, illegal federal funding freezes, and more

body:
only two weeks in.

|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101501416/526147323/1674156104?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_2Weeks_250203_T1_2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODkzNjNmYy04YWUyLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk3ZTkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q745cumoYnUBIwmvFHszxxw_3AhoRraY4JXfFkOkDkY=) Chaos, Derek.

That’s a fair way of describing Trump’s first two weeks in office.

Whether he’s putting tariffs on goods and making a lot of the things you buy – we’re talking American cars, sneakers, and avocados – more expensive or going back-and-forth on his illegal funding freeze, he’s completely ignoring the turmoil he inflicts on Americans in the process.

[_**No matter how much confusion and chaos he sows, rest assured, I’ll be fighting back every step of the way to make our communities stronger and safer. Chip in now if you’re with me >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101501417/526147329/1674156104?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_2Weeks_250203_T1_2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODkzNjNmYy04YWUyLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk3ZTkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q745cumoYnUBIwmvFHszxxw_3AhoRraY4JXfFkOkDkY=)

It’s going to be a long, bumpy road, Derek, but it needs to be taken.

Candidly, there’s no “getting around” a Trump presidency. We just have to get through it. We have to stand together and take action when he or his administration do anything to hurt the livelihoods of folks at home in VA and across the nation.

**And when we have an opportunity, we have to grab it.** Our next big opportunity to stand up against Trump and the GOP majority in Washington is in 2026, where we can take back the Senate and regain Democratic momentum in Congress.

[_**Will you donate today to support my re-election campaign and help me flip the Senate blue so we can bring back a line of defense against Trump and the GOP?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101501418/526147332/1674156104?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_2Weeks_250203_T1_2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODkzNjNmYy04YWUyLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk3ZTkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q745cumoYnUBIwmvFHszxxw_3AhoRraY4JXfFkOkDkY=)

I really appreciate your support.

\- Mark
****[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101501422/526147335/1674156104?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_2Weeks_250203_T1_2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODkzNjNmYy04YWUyLWVmMTEtODhmOC0wMDIyNDgyYTk3ZTkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q745cumoYnUBIwmvFHszxxw_3AhoRraY4JXfFkOkDkY=)****

name: Supervisor David Canepa
subject: February 2025 e-newsletter
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us1.campaign-archive.com/?e=c0babbc643&u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=9c9da8200b)
---
| | | |
---
|
---
| | | | |
#### e-newsletter

## FEB '25
---
|
* **$750,000 grant to support youth programming at Daly City Partnership**
* **I’m honored to serve as San Mateo County President for 2025**
* **Chamber fireside chat focuses on future of affordable housing**
* **County prepares for federal policy changes**
* **Ferry service clears hurdle to operate at Port of Redwood City**
* **Students get grand tour of County Center**
* **Interns wanted to join our team**

---
| | |
---
|
# **“** The Redwood City Port and waterfront is a jewel and electric ferry service connecting the Peninsula to San Francisco and Oakland will expand economic growth and opportunity along this vital corridor in Silicon Valley in an environmentally friendly way**”**
# **\- President David Canepa**
---
| | | | ******I’m honored to serve as San Mateo County President for 2025** Dear friends, I was unanimously chosen by the Board of Supervisors Jan. 7 to serve as President for 2025 and am thankful to Vice President Noelia Corzo, Supervisor Ray Mueller and our new members Lisa Gauthier and Jackie Speier for having the faith in me to lead the Board as a new administration leads the White House. I shared my vision during my acceptance speech for a county grounded in collaboration and common-sense governance to guide decision-making about, among other priorities, affordable housing, the environment, health, the economy and equity. I also compared the fear and unknowns about the new federal administration to those the county faced with innovation and compassion during the Covid-19 pandemic. I said the county will not quietly accept changes and actions that harm our community. We will navigate these potentially choppy waters ahead to ensure everyone who lives and works here is treated with human decency and respect.” I was proud to be sworn into office by California State Controller Malia Cohen as I was accompanied by my wife Ana and son Piero.

**Chamber fireside chat focuses on future of affordable housing**

Thank you to the Burlingame/SFO Chamber of Commerce for inviting me to discuss San Mateo County’s commitment to address the housing crisis at its annual meeting and luncheon Thursday, Jan. 23.

As the President of the Board of Supervisors for 2025, I’m proud to say the county has committed more than $300 million since 2012 to combat the crisis by financing nearly 5,000 affordable homes which include projects such as Eucalyptus Grove in Burlingame, Middlefield Junction in North Fair Oaks and the transformative Midway Village in Daly City.

The high cost of living in San Mateo County places enormous strain on families, individuals, and our workforce. We are the wealthiest county in the state and third wealthiest in the entire nation with median home prices of $2.4 million. I told the packed audience at the Hyatt Regency in Burlingame that I am also definitely interested in expanding investments in workforce housing and do think there will be opportunities to build on public lands.

A big thank you to Burlingame City Manager Lisa Goldman, Mayor Peter Stevenson, the city council, David Burruto with Assemblymember Diane Papan’s office and Chamber President Susan Baker for your unwavering leadership and for hosting a very lively and engaging event.

**County prepares for federal policy changes**
I joined civic leaders from across San Mateo County Thursday Jan. 16 to prepare for expected sweeping changes to state and federal policies and priorities. Uncertainty surrounds the future of health care funding and eligibility, immigration policy, infrastructure investment and more and it has potential significant impacts for the county, our cites and nonprofit partners. The day-long conference at the San Mateo County Event Center was attended by about 150 representatives from cities, nonprofit organizations and other groups was a listening session by the county to understand concerns and how changes to federal policy might influence critical services provided to county residents. Participants such as Shareen Leland with StarVista, Rita Mancera with Puente de las Costa Sur, Heather Cleary with Peninsula Family Service and Laura Bent with Samaritan House (pictured above with conference moderator Rosanne Foust with SAMCEDA) said they were concerned that changes to the eligibility for a host of programs funded by the federal government could create a chilling effect, stopping families from applying for or receiving certain benefits. Discussions focused on ways local governments and community groups can come together to support a wide array of safety-net services, regardless of who delivers those services.

**Ferry service clears hurdle to operate at Port of Redwood City**

The Redwood City Ferry Project got a boost from the Metropolitan Transportation Commission, which I serve on, when the full MTC Board of Commissioners voted Wednesday Jan. 22 to add the project to Plan Bay Area 50+, which makes the project eligible for federal funding.

This game-changing project will connect the Port of Redwood City to Oakland and San Francisco, driving economic growth and opportunity along the Peninsula in an environmentally friendly way. As a certified federal staging area, it also strengthens our region’s emergency preparedness for natural disasters.

The San Mateo County Transportation Authority has pitched in $15 million for the project and the state has provided an additional $5 million

Thank you to Kristine Zortman, the Executive Director of the Port of Redwood City, fellow MTC Commissioner Gina Papan, Michael Quigley, Executive Director of the California Alliance for Jobs and so many others for your advocacy on a project that will create lots of good jobs and boost the regional economy.

**$750,000 grant to support youth programming at Daly City Partnership** The Daly City Partnership was awarded a $750,000 by the Board of Supervisors Jan. 28 to support youth after school programming and community mental health services in Northern San Mateo County through January 2028. This agreement support essential services for youth and families, as well as mental health programs that have been significantly impacted by funding reductions. Specifically, the funding will enable the continuation of afterschool, summer, and school holiday programs at Our Second Home on the JFK Elementary School campus. These programs provide daily homework assistance, tutoring and weekly STEAM enrichment activities. Additionally, the funding will support a range of mental health services, including group, family, couple and individual therapy.

**Students get grand tour of County Center** I had the opportunity to host a tour for more than 100 awesome 5th graders from Woodrow Wilson, Westlake and George Washington elementary schools around the county government campus in downtown Redwood City Wednesday, Jan. 29, including our new Board Chambers located at 555 County Center, the nation’s greenest civic building. The students spent the morning learning about the Fred Korematsu Day of Civil Liberties presented by the San Mateo County Office of Education’s Department of Expanded Learning and Community Partnerships. The annual Fred Korematsu Day of Civil Liberties and the Constitution, established in California in 2010, honors his legacy and promotes education about civil liberties. Fred Korematsu was a Japanese American civil rights activist who played a significant role in challenging the internment of Japanese Americans during World War II. If you or your school are interested in a tour, please contact our staff at (650) 363-4572. **Interns wanted to join our team ** We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: [https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=0de55ea460&e=c0babbc643 "https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9?fbclid=IwAR3YFH0mH828rtAUru5EFOO9GsMCasQYe1N7AUrmwAwBf30JTtXOSeCzKjs"). You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at [mrichardson@smcgov.org](mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org "mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org"). In friendship,
**DAVID J. CANEPA**
President, San Mateo County Board of Supervisors
---
| | | |
---

Students get grand tour of County Center I had the opportunity to host a tour for more than 100 awesome 5th graders from Woodrow Wilson, Westlake and George Washington elementary schools around the county government campus in downtown Redwood City Wednesday, Jan. 29, including our new Board Chambers located at 555 County Center, the nation’s greenest civic building. The students spent the morning learning about the Fred Korematsu Day of Civil Liberties presented by the San Mateo County Office of Education’s Department of Expanded Learning and Community Partnerships. The annual Fred Korematsu Day of Civil Liberties and the Constitution, established in California in 2010, honors his legacy and promotes education about civil liberties. Fred Korematsu was a Japanese American civil rights activist who played a significant role in challenging the internment of Japanese Americans during World War II. If you or your school are interested in a tour, please contact our staff at (650) 363-4572. Interns wanted to join our team We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: "". You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org "mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org". In friendship, DAVID J. CANEPA President, San Mateo County Board of Supervisors | | | | | Copyright © 2025 Supervisor David Canepa, All rights reserved.

Here's what's on the docket, Peter:
**🏛️ Fighting to elect pro-choice women to the courts! Justice Allison Riggs** in North Carolina won her state Supreme Court election in November, but Republicans are still trying to overturn the election results. We're continuing to put pressure on Judge Griffin to concede. Meanwhile, in Wisconsin, the race to hold the progressive state Supreme Court majority is just two months away, and we're supporting**Judge Susan Crawford** in this crucial election.

🗳️ **More endorsements coming soon!** Democratic pro-choice candidates are stepping up to run for office every day, Peter. We recently endorsed **Jocelyn Benson** in the Michigan governors race, and there's more to come in the coming weeks — stay tuned!

**📣 Uplifting EMILYs List women and calling out anti-choice extremists.** Senate and House Republicans are falling in line with President Trump's dangerous agenda to restrict abortion access nationwide. We'll continue to call MAGA extremism out and fight for the future of reproductive rights. Our candidates are central to this fight.

Make no mistake: the road ahead will be tough, and Republicans will pull out all the stops to get their way. But with the continued support of this community, we're ready to face every challenge — and win.

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email02032025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email02032025&amount=25]
Peter,
We’re not going to sugarcoat it—our country is in a full-blown constitutional crisis. Trump is pushing every boundary, Senate Republicans are rubber-stamping his worst impulses, and billionaires are inserting themselves directly into government operations. Here’s where things stand:
Trump’s Tariff Disaster Begins
In his never-ending quest to reinvent economic sense into chaos, Trump has unleashed tariffs on imports from China, Canada, and Mexico—because, of course, nothing says “national security” like punishing two of your closest allies. Analysts are warning that these tariffs will force higher prices on American consumers, hitting everything from automobiles to electronics. The auto industry alone could see a staggering $33 billion in annual cost increases due to these import expenses. And just when you thought it couldn’t get any more farcical, Canada has decided to push back by planning retaliatory tariffs on $105 billion worth of U.S. goods, setting the stage for an international trade spat that will leave everyday Americans footing the bill.
Musk Runs Amok in the Federal Government
In an unprecedented abuse of power, Elon Musk has been given free rein to interrupt Treasury Department and USAID operations. He’s reportedly interfering with how Congress-allocated funds are distributed, something no private citizen should have the authority to do. This is not just corruption—it’s Trump handing over key government functions to a billionaire ally with no accountability.
Senate Moves Full Steam Ahead on RFK Jr. and Tulsi Gabbard
Despite major red flags, Senate Republicans are moving forward with the confirmations of RFK Jr. and Tulsi Gabbard—two of Trump’s most unhinged, unqualified picks. These nominees are extreme, dangerous, and have no business shaping American policy, but the GOP is proving once again that a majority of their caucus would rather fall in line than provide oversight.
Let’s Be Clear: We Are in a Constitutional Crisis
This is not how our government is supposed to function. Trump is pulling every lever in sight, testing the strength of our systems, and seeing how far he can go. The stakes are too high for us to sit back and hope it all works out.
So what do we do? We fight smarter. That starts with: Winning every election possible in 2025 to put strong civil servants in office and talking to voters now about why Trump’s policies aren’t normal and won’t help them. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email02032025&amount=25]
We need to build early, build strong, and build everywhere. If you’re ready to fight back, chip in now to help us elect the right leaders and push back against Trump’s abuses of power. We’ve already held several seats in special elections in Minnesota and Virginia this year, you can help fuel our next wins.
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email02032025&amount=25]
Let's get to work!
GOTV PAC
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email12082024&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email12082024&amount=25]
Get Out the Vote PAC mobilizes voters often overlooked by mainstream campaigns to help Democratic candidates win in the most competitive districts.
Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member.
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email01272025&amount=25]
Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: I want to hear from you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
I don’t care if you’re a Democrat, Republican, or Independent — I’ll listen to and work with anyone to build a better Michigan.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=73%2E181023%2ENC7qnO)
Team — I’m running to be the next governor of Michigan because I want to take _your_ stories, _your_ concerns, _your_ struggles, and your successes with me to Lansing.
**As I travel across the state, I’m all ears. As I share what I see for the future of Michigan, I’m incorporating your thoughts and stories.**
I wish I could meet each and every one of you on the campaign trail! I love nothing more than getting to know the people who make Michigan home.
**[I hope to see you on the campaign trail, but in the meantime, can you take a few moments to fill out our campaign’s priorities survey? I want to hear how I can best serve you as Michigan’s next governor.](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/survey/2025-Priorities-Survey?refcode=AU_JMB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250203_PM1_V1_S1&email=&akid=73%2E181023%2ENC7qnO)**
**[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/survey/2025-Priorities-Survey?refcode=AU_JMB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250203_PM1_V1_S1&email=&akid=73%2E181023%2ENC7qnO)**
My goal is to make everyone’s life a little bit easier so that our state is the best place to be a kid, raise a kid, and call home.
I don’t care if you’re a Democrat, Republican, or Independent — I’ll listen to and work with anyone to build a better Michigan.
The conversations I have with voters are extraordinary. I believe the best way to learn about the issues is to show up where people are.
As I travel across Michigan, I’m taking all I learn and turning it into real plans to make a difference in our state.
I wish I could meet every one of you on the campaign trail to hear your stories and how I can help. **[The next best thing you can do is share your thoughts with me in our first priorities survey of this race. Take a few minutes to fill out the survey and let me know what’s on your mind.](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/survey/2025-Priorities-Survey?refcode=AU_JMB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250203_PM1_V1_S1&email=&akid=73%2E181023%2ENC7qnO)**
**[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/survey/2025-Priorities-Survey?refcode=AU_JMB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250203_PM1_V1_S1&email=&akid=73%2E181023%2ENC7qnO)**
I’m listening and learning — and I can’t wait to hear from you.
Thank you!
— Jocelyn

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-jbmi/?t=2&akid=73%2E181023%2ENC7qnO)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/update-your-information-jbmi/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-jbmi/?t=3&akid=73%2E181023%2ENC7qnO)

PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR

23133 Woodward Ave, Number 116,
Ferndale, MI 48220

body:
Grateful to say that we met our goal

Gabe Vasquez won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022. He's running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. Gracias, friends. On Friday, we faced our first end-of-month fundraising deadline since Donald Trump was inaugurated for a second time, and I am grateful to say that we met our goal I can’t emphasize this enough: Your grassroots support has been the beating heart of this campaign, and I am so thankful that I have folks like you by my side, especially as we’ve witnessed the chaos Donald Trump has caused in the first weeks of his second presidency. These are unprecedented times, and I know that many of us are struggling to process the President’s blatant disregard for the Constitution he took an oath to protect. But it is heartening to know that folks like you who helped us hold NM-02 blue are ready to keep fighting — **because I am too.** We have a lot of work ahead of us as we prepare to defend our democracy at all costs, and it’s more critical than ever that we keep our momentum high. So I need to ask: [Will you chip in today and help us kickstart our fundraising efforts for February? He's running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Gracias, friends. On Friday, we faced our first end-of-month fundraising deadline since Donald Trump was inaugurated for a second time, and I am grateful to say that we met our goal I can’t emphasize this enough: Your grassroots support has been the beating heart of this campaign, and I am so thankful that I have folks like you by my side, especially as we’ve witnessed the chaos Donald Trump has caused in the first weeks of his second presidency. These are unprecedented times, and I know that many of us are struggling to process the President’s blatant disregard for the Constitution he took an oath to protect. But it is heartening to know that folks like you who helped us hold NM-02 blue are ready to keep fighting — because I am too. We have a lot of work ahead of us as we prepare to defend our democracy at all costs, and it’s more critical than ever that we keep our momentum high. name: Lacy Lunsford
subject: I’m writing to you today as a last resort
body:
My husband is an honorable man who wanted nothing more than to serve his country and his community.| | | my husband had no choice -- it was the suspect’s life or his partner’s.
---
| |
---
Dear Patriot,My husband saved the life of his fellow officer and now, on February 3rd, a Soros-funded prosecutor is going to try and lock him up for _11 years_.
Here is one of my favorite pictures of our two boys.
---
[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=4f7531a0d7794ac191f941489004710c&_e=Ixq_P7GHHCMkah7gGf3uNYeygzyyi2-WT3I4C7W-jxlrWGX4EwGtfaKdFz-siBRQFJigm6F8LMK2HARs1nUT35PeJcHIxgmL0gQAbntWr5piOuM_DMfHt925NRwkWdSVn9EDiWKp3nk9y_Eb0iZ5pmw022kT_xglqwE21lZvxNWE_VkHgQ3pSb0vHuhfoi-a6TEnUsEIvY6KhPebFKSacQ_xPIjz5HvNd43MajGwxKxx0cl2uiIuJzkVBz9uN_TOgMYUaSBi2r3_7jwlgr4UAFAOgB-gpnY6AzJNP3MpLlpKIe6UJExcpVWv881vlEGe1UOhvUwkgkrNMMTB9_F2jnVQiMF-PMIkvt4_1MKEjcT_R12F-slsbdMo9IecDuX0)
---
[](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=4f7531a0d7794ac191f941489004710c&_e=Ixq_P7GHHCMkah7gGf3uNYeygzyyi2-WT3I4C7W-jxlrWGX4EwGtfaKdFz-siBRQFJigm6F8LMK2HARs1nUT35PeJcHIxgmL0gQAbntWr5piOuM_DMfHt925NRwkWdSVn9EDiWKp3nk9y_Eb0iZ5pmw022kT_xglqwE21lZvxNWE_VkHgQ3pSb0vHuhfoi-a6TEnUsEIvY6KhPebFKSacQ_xPIjz5HvNd43MajGwxKw_GidL-5UbnYtAM_W_1bfizBEZhsDGnG06aPl93h8LHr8hCxfwX0PwzR71hVksFZW5lYXsQmmX82QZaFJkh_0tibBe0G3sKV9g1JbIa32sHTi_P5upVDnReooockLHi60za9_i5i1gyHul-6AFQea6)
---
Now it makes me cry. As you can probably tell from my sons' costumes, my husband Brad is a proud U.S. Army veteran. He served two tours of duty in Iraq as a Cavalry Scout. And today he is also a decorated, 9-year veteran of the Las Cruces, New Mexico, police department. I'm used to my husband being in danger. But never in my wildest dreams did I think the biggest threat to Brad wouldn't come from a radical terrorist or a violent gangster... ...but from a _Soros-funded_ , _anti-police_ _prosecutor_. **[**That's why I'm writing you today as a last resort to ask if you'll help me fight for my husband.**](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=4f7531a0d7794ac191f941489004710c&_e=Ixq_P7GHHCMkah7gGf3uNYeygzyyi2-WT3I4C7W-jxlrWGX4EwGtfaKdFz-siBRQFJigm6F8LMK2HARs1nUT35PeJcHIxgmL0gQAbntWr5piOuM_DMfHt925NRwkWdSVn9EDiWKp3nk9y_Eb0iZ5pmw022kT_xglqwE21lZvxNWE_VkHgQ3pSb0vHuhfoi-a6TEnUsEIvY6KhPebFKSacQ_xPIjz5HvNd43MajGwxKyrZThdlY3O4pgDE9dqx00cmoI3Jje7q6ARrp84UcqkUHY8O1zCqwKcW5Ad_R0ChVJRCutIyYHUxK0Ilfe8hFhiBgU48gVo94TJE_w3IhyxI9NlSN88bqSe4AsTN4B-iw9u__Tcbxf2uGrOU5Lz4PeN)** Our nightmare started the day my husband responded to a shoplifting call. When Brad arrived on the scene, right away he sensed something was off. And when he and his partner, Officer Keegan Arbogast, asked the suspect to identify himself, the suspect refused. So when Officer Arbogast saw a knife sitting on the suspect's lap, hidden under a water bottle, the officers moved to detain him. Instead of complying, the suspect got violent. Everything that happened next happened in an instant. As the officers tried to subdue him, the suspect went for Officer Arbogast's gun, trying to pull it from the holster. Then he lunged for Officer Arbogast's taser, yanked it free, and pointed it directly at him. In that moment, my husband had no choice -- it was the suspect's life or his partner's. He drew his pistol and fired, killing the suspect and saving Officer Arbogast's life. When one of Brad's coworkers called me to tell me there had been a shooting, I burst into tears. I didn't stop crying until Brad came home. I was so thankful he was safe. I knew he did what he had to do to protect his partner so they could both make it home to their families. And when an Internal Investigation _and_ the local District Attorney cleared my husband of any wrongdoing, I thought this awful episode was behind us. I couldn't have been more wrong. Because more than a year after the incident, the Attorney General of New Mexico charged my husband with Voluntary Manslaughter! When I heard about the charges, I was shocked. Then we did some digging -- and it all made sense. The New Mexico Attorney General is a rabid far-Left activist who _hates_ the police. He was funded by George Soros. And because my husband is white, and the suspect was black... ...this radical prosecutor is determined to make a name for himself by destroying my husband and dragging his name through the mud. He even held a press conference and turned the microphone over to the suspect's family so they could spread hateful lies about my husband, smearing him as a "racist" and a "cold-blooded killer." **Make no mistake -- these charges are a slap in the face to every brave man and woman who has ever put on the badge.** Now you can see why I'm so heartbroken and furious. My husband is an honorable man who wanted nothing more than to serve his country and his community. That's why he served for 3 years in the Army as a cavalry scout. And it's why he served for 10 years as a police officer. But more than that, he's a wonderful husband and an incredible father to our 9- and 6-year-old boys. We've tried to explain to our boys as best we can about what's going on. But they're too young to fully understand. And I can't bear to think of what it will do to them if one day, Daddy is taken away and they're forced to grow up with their father behind bars. But unless people like you speak up to defend my husband against these disgusting Soros-funded political attacks... ...Brad will miss 11 years of birthdays, wedding anniversaries, and Little League games. As I mentioned earlier, Brad's trial starts in less than two weeks and already, the legal bills have become overwhelming for our family. Thank God, we got a call from Jason Johnson. Jason is a retired police officer -- and he's the President of the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund (LELDF). If you've ever heard of LELDF, you know they're a national group fighting on behalf of wrongly-accused police officers like Brad. LELDF has helped dozens of innocent officers -- and their families -- when it seemed like there was no hope left. And with the thought of losing my husband and trying to suddenly raise my two boys on my own -- I was certainly hopeless. Not only did LELDF decide to help my husband get the legal defense he deserves, they're also raising money to pay for the best lawyers and expert witnesses available. But it’s only because of the support of brave patriots like yourself. Jason and LELDF were an answer to prayer for our family. That's because Brad and I aren't wealthy people. I run our family farm and Brad is a veteran turned cop. So I must ask you one of the toughest questions I've ever asked anyone. **[**Will you donate $35 or any amount to LELDF to help me save my husband?**](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=4f7531a0d7794ac191f941489004710c&_e=Ixq_P7GHHCMkah7gGf3uNYeygzyyi2-WT3I4C7W-jxlrWGX4EwGtfaKdFz-siBRQFJigm6F8LMK2HARs1nUT35PeJcHIxgmL0gQAbntWr5piOuM_DMfHt925NRwkWdSVn9EDiWKp3nk9y_Eb0iZ5pmw022kT_xglqwE21lZvxNWE_VkHgQ3pSb0vHuhfoi-a6TEnUsEIvY6KhPebFKSacQ_xPIjz5HvNd43MajGwxKxBjNZz-xW7QzKuWmUjtgMhkOTB1A0tUJSnnyDbIS9JJBWPfJIGlugtO8d7nyzxoNc3nDXOR_cABT_LHvDaO_F-P_n8cg4HZws1iXr_RBtGoXJduLAG6Md5ZJlicoD10jnqj9wp6hFpbM4JUWk0ZIjV)** If I had anywhere else to turn, I wouldn't be so bold as to ask for your help. But I'm so scared for Brad and I will do whatever I can for him -- and for our sons. **[So I pray you'll find it in your heart today to make a tax-deductible gift of $35, $50, $100 or more to the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund.](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=4f7531a0d7794ac191f941489004710c&_e=Ixq_P7GHHCMkah7gGf3uNYeygzyyi2-WT3I4C7W-jxlrWGX4EwGtfaKdFz-siBRQFJigm6F8LMK2HARs1nUT35PeJcHIxgmL0gQAbntWr5piOuM_DMfHt925NRwkWdSVn9EDiWKp3nk9y_Eb0iZ5pmw022kT_xglqwE21lZvxNWE_VkHgQ3pSb0vHuhfoi-a6TEnUsEIvY6KhPebFKSacQ_xPIjz5HvNd43MajGwxKwGw9VfJQ1vBzKgvKVJn7SuUDO7I9YAcEjFl-JbNQ0k4juwjrhCQXHBeZ8Lt_w96euk1227tqR4Kn--2K5-n8T4vua_I6n0QBL9xk7yG9GJJ4XA2hki_NxaADKMKXLU0YuhskIPaIQRjPxdZPpWoz3m) ** Brad and I -- and our two sons -- are counting on the good nature and generosity of kind neighbors like you to help keep Brad home where he belongs. No matter what you're able to give today, please pray for us. Thank you in advance for your support.
---
Sincerely,
---
Lacy Lunsford
---
| | P.S. I already spent a year apart from my husband when he was in the Army. It's the hardest thing I've ever done. He almost died during that deployment when the Humvee he was driving hit an IED. I can't imagine having to spend 11 long, lonely years, trying to raise the boys by myself -- and worrying every day about my husband, locked up with hundreds of violent criminals. Please don't let my two boys grow up without their father -- [**please follow this link right now to make a tax-deductible donation of $35 or any amount to the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund today.**](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=4f7531a0d7794ac191f941489004710c&_e=Ixq_P7GHHCMkah7gGf3uNYeygzyyi2-WT3I4C7W-jxlrWGX4EwGtfaKdFz-siBRQFJigm6F8LMK2HARs1nUT35PeJcHIxgmL0gQAbntWr5piOuM_DMfHt925NRwkWdSVn9EDiWKp3nk9y_Eb0iZ5pmw022kT_xglqwE21lZvxNWE_VkHgQ3pSb0vHuhfoi-a6TEnUsEIvY6KhPebFKSacQ_xPIjz5HvNd43MajGwxKwMQIIZw2EHagu9PPOPOFXZ7SGtCHPkVjp9zGL1gp4OZ-ETKk2HTlBB5Ofsj0mGm8C6xPL-Vbx3xN5eVveaTcNRtHkst94qDFbWNu7Z-_zWiDjOlzJv-cXcSDZzz2s03ZchQyirFHUkL1Zcs194OtDk) P.P.S. Brad's trial starts on February 3rd, so we're quickly running out time. Thank you for helping my family fight back against this Soros-funded prosecutor!
---
|
---
| | _More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit. And remember –Blue Lives Matter!_
---

Then we did some digging -- and it all made sense. The New Mexico Attorney General is a rabid far-Left activist who hates the police. He was funded by George Soros. And because my husband is white, and the suspect was black... ...this radical prosecutor is determined to make a name for himself by destroying my husband and dragging his name through the mud. He even held a press conference and turned the microphone over to the suspect's family so they could spread hateful lies about my husband, smearing him as a "racist" and a "cold-blooded killer." Make no mistake -- these charges are a slap in the face to every brave man and woman who has ever put on the badge. Now you can see why I'm so heartbroken and furious. My husband is an honorable man who wanted nothing more than to serve his country and his community. That's why he served for 3 years in the Army as a cavalry scout. And it's why he served for 10 years as a police officer. But more than that, he's a wonderful husband and an incredible father to our 9- and 6-year-old boys. We've tried to explain to our boys as best we can about what's going on. But they're too young to fully understand. And I can't bear to think of what it will do to them if one day, Daddy is taken away and they're forced to grow up with their father behind bars. But unless people like you speak up to defend my husband against these disgusting Soros-funded political attacks... ...Brad will miss 11 years of birthdays, wedding anniversaries, and Little League games. As I mentioned earlier, Brad's trial starts in less than two weeks and already, the legal bills have become overwhelming for our family. Thank God, we got a call from Jason Johnson. Jason is a retired police officer -- and he's the President of the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund (LELDF). If you've ever heard of LELDF, you know they're a national group fighting on behalf of wrongly-accused police officers like Brad. LELDF has helped dozens of innocent officers -- and their families -- when it seemed like there was no hope left. And with the thought of losing my husband and trying to suddenly raise my two boys on my own -- I was certainly hopeless. Not only did LELDF decide to help my husband get the legal defense he deserves, they're also raising money to pay for the best lawyers and expert witnesses available. But it’s only because of the support of brave patriots like yourself. Jason and LELDF were an answer to prayer for our family. That's because Brad and I aren't wealthy people. I run our family farm and Brad is a veteran turned cop. So I must ask you one of the toughest questions I've ever asked anyone. If I had anywhere else to turn, I wouldn't be so bold as to ask for your help. But I'm so scared for Brad and I will do whatever I can for him -- and for our sons. Brad and I -- and our two sons -- are counting on the good nature and generosity of kind neighbors like you to help keep Brad home where he belongs. No matter what you're able to give today, please pray for us. Thank you in advance for your support. Sincerely, Lacy Lunsford | | P.S. I already spent a year apart from my husband when he was in the Army. It's the hardest thing I've ever done. He almost died during that deployment when the Humvee he was driving hit an IED. I can't imagine having to spend 11 long, lonely years, trying to raise the boys by myself -- and worrying every day about my husband, locked up with hundreds of violent criminals. Please don't let my two boys grow up without their father -- P.P.S. Brad's trial starts on February 3rd, so we're quickly running out time. Thank you for helping my family fight back against this Soros-funded prosecutor! | | | More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit. And remember –Blue Lives Matter! © Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | 2560 Huntington Ave. Suite 203, Alexandria, VA, 22303

name: Bryan
subject: February 3 Campaign Update
body:
With President Trump in the White House, Republicans have a real opportunity to pass election integrity legislation and get . . .

Voter Action Project Logo [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=020325_3&amount=25]
Fox 5 Atlanta: Georgia secretary of state calls for end of general election runoffs [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/F5ATL_EndRunoffs.png]
Georgia Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger has said that he wants the Georgia General Assembly to end the Peach State's use of general election runoffs. In a statement, Raffensperger called on Georgia officials to get rid of the runoff process, which costs taxpayers millions of dollars each time it has to be used.
Historians have found that most general election runoff systems were designed to limit participation, specifically of minority groups.
We want to hear from voters with their thoughts on this. Will you take our one-question poll right away?
Team: Should states eliminate the runoff system and declare the candidate with the most votes from the general election the winner?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=020325_3&amount=15] NO [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=020325_3&amount=15]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=020325_3&amount=25]

body:
Sharing some of the powerful words from your fellow community members >>
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101503377/526180866/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ0YmU3MjNhLTkzZTItZWYxMS04OGY4LTAwMjI0ODJhOTdlOSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=NkBpai00UiR4CHmmEBMfdGDNAkiIa8WcRJpk8b4SPC0=&emci=056367bd-57e2-ef11-88f8-0022482a97e9&emdi=44be723a-93e2-ef11-88f8-0022482a97e9&ceid=8431045)
---

Wow, Peter! A few weeks ago, we asked this community how you want to further our shared mission in 2025 of electing pro-democracy leaders, protecting fair maps, and combating ongoing gerrymandering. **_Hundreds_ of people weighed in — and I want to share a few of your powerful responses:** | **Why are you in this fight for fair maps?**
---
“Because I believe in one person one vote and not having corporations drive America. The concept of fairness learned in kindergarten is still my golden rule.” — _Doreen T._ “I am in this fight because I am afraid for this country and I would like to surround myself with like-minded people who want to turn the tide together.“ — _Thomas P._ “I hope it would mean a more equal distribution of wealth and access to housing, food, and health care.” — _Lillian L._ “Basic fairness. It burns me up that legislatures choose their voters rather than vice versa.” — _Johanna M._ “Fair maps are a prerequisite for a truly representative democracy. Competitive districts are the key because elected officials who represent competitive districts must be responsive to their constituents.” — _Jay S._
We’re so grateful to everyone in this community who responded. We’re reading every answer and using your feedback to shape this work in the months to come.
[**Want to do something to help drive this work forward _right now_? Make a gift of $30 or more today.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101503378/526180867/-1665762292?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_SurveyReportBack_email_20250203-NDRC-Tier1-SurveyReportBack_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250203-NDRC-Tier1-SurveyReportBack_&utm_campaign=SurveyReportBack&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ0YmU3MjNhLTkzZTItZWYxMS04OGY4LTAwMjI0ODJhOTdlOSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=NkBpai00UiR4CHmmEBMfdGDNAkiIa8WcRJpk8b4SPC0=)
Thank you,
Vic
Senior Mobilization Strategist

clean_body: Sharing some of the powerful words from your fellow community members >> | Wow, Peter! A few weeks ago, we asked this community how you want to further our shared mission in 2025 of electing pro-democracy leaders, protecting fair maps, and combating ongoing gerrymandering. Hundreds of people weighed in — and I want to share a few of your powerful responses: | Why are you in this fight for fair maps? “Because I believe in one person one vote and not having corporations drive America. The concept of fairness learned in kindergarten is still my golden rule.” — Doreen T. “I am in this fight because I am afraid for this country and I would like to surround myself with like-minded people who want to turn the tide together.“ — Thomas P. “I hope it would mean a more equal distribution of wealth and access to housing, food, and health care.” — Lillian L. “Basic fairness. It burns me up that legislatures choose their voters rather than vice versa.” — Johanna M. “Fair maps are a prerequisite for a truly representative democracy. Competitive districts are the key because elected officials who represent competitive districts must be responsive to their constituents.” — Jay S. We’re so grateful to everyone in this community who responded. We’re reading every answer and using your feedback to shape this work in the months to come. = Thank you, Vic Senior Mobilization Strategist = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. Every dollar you give will go toward our fight to end gerrymandering and voter suppression. | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC.

Dear Peter,
As thousands gathered in D.C. for the March For Life, I felt an overwhelming sense of unity and hope. The sight of women, men, young people, and children rallying together for the sanctity of life was truly inspiring.

[I STAND FOR LIFE](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/SHNU9_kBMnnHTdytLxGASnWh7k2vyoSPUaRzy_1x_87iNWBee5TU25uKTHQv5CXNEq2Q0vdzM0_kps4q1PioppsMd_OMxKW7I2H1FQKz8tMs3J_MPloYsHK_KwgJPJfBCGnG3IRzts8t3QXvpMtCZF7jzFnKdnjEba67lbXT9trdVv-dOGgwgk0wXpLfZG68TlB7hftTa_b1sV7j4zcfyqtFWWEUB8G6uZlcS_MGFgk1YPw9KG21Xn_VcUR87SK2Ly_SZht7wVIYIMuuujyuLBKxMouOtbohTz2Ozj2WoWFBg9ir6CishNMvL8QkkniryeGnIB7_gm0LmkOudiSjxJo5wAElNhyXmtlt29tpMreYTZhkAoiB7C8k3jtkHHCky3u0DyiQp4DWMpUMW-bqqgRB9uVprhwDHDANC6Q2ovxR9z3NlooIIpM6JUPktMygAQZd9gJnHnwbkfDDpAnW-YWQa7UukCfEeSfdBBhr/9zXG6vChrv-y05gsKQEf4QTPjXMch-dF/5)

name: Senator Jennifer Boysko
subject: Boysko Bulletin: Budget Reports Published As Crossover Nears
body:
| | |
---
**Boysko Bulletin: ****Budget Reports Published As Crossover Nears**** **
---
Dear Peter, Sunday afternoon, I returned to Richmond early for a Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee meeting. After several weeks of reviewing budget amendments for legislation, requests for amendments unrelated to bills, and the Governor’s budget amendments, we released our [subcommittee reports](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101503788/526183329/-955736802?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmM5MTVlMTEtOTRlMi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5N2U5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=FWS1yuMmZYIXK5q2r4aMGd_i_iWxr-uX0yveJKNsASE=&emci=fe26d897-b8dc-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=bc915e11-94e2-ef11-88f8-0022482a97e9&ceid=22486556). The House of Delegates also released their [subcommittee reports](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101503790/526183331/-955736802?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmM5MTVlMTEtOTRlMi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5N2U5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=FWS1yuMmZYIXK5q2r4aMGd_i_iWxr-uX0yveJKNsASE=&emci=fe26d897-b8dc-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=bc915e11-94e2-ef11-88f8-0022482a97e9&ceid=22486556). As each Chamber moves forward this week to formally adopt its respective budget, the two versions will go to a conference committee made up of representatives from both bodies, who will work to develop a compromise budget. It is important to note that while the state has a significant surplus of over $2.5 billion in discretionary funding this year, the majority of those funds are non-recurring and cannot be used to meet ongoing needs. In contrast with the Governor’s proposed budget, the Senate’s focus is clear, to ensure that hardworking families keep more of the money they earn. The Senate budget rejects the Governor’s phased car tax rebates totaling $1.1 billion in favor of tax changes that will benefit more Virginians this year. These include one-time income tax rebates of $200 for single filers and $400 for joint filers, reducing tax liabilities by increasing the standard deduction amounts to $8,750/$17,500, and raising the state Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) to 20% of the federal level. The state EITC is a proven anti-poverty program. The Governor’s budget proposal would let this key tax credit for working families expire, which would raise taxes on these earners. We need state and federal tax policies that ensure hardworking people can keep more of what they earn. Corporations and the ultra-wealthy must pay their fair share toward funding the right priorities, such as housing, health care and child care. Corporations and wealthy CEOs hold too much power in this country. I’m here to fight for you, not the rich special interests that are keeping prices high. The Senate budget provides more than $400 million in additional direct aid increases for K-12 schools. This includes funding for big-ticket items, including fully lifting the support cap, creating a new state add-on for special education students, and providing the state share of $1,000 bonuses for school staff. While I would like to see ongoing salary increases, we did not have the recurring funds available this year. I fought for and am pleased that we fully lifted the support cap, which has reduced funding for critical support staff positions (instructional aides, cafeteria workers, custodians, etc.) in schools since the 2009 recession. In response to the recent JLARC study, we added funding for special education students. I will continue to oppose the Governor’s efforts to shift public education funding to private schools. The Senate removed the $50 million the Governor allocated for private and religious school vouchers. These types of programs have been shown to harm student outcomes for those who participate while also diverting critical resources away from public schools. We removed more than $60 million from the Governor’s budget for a new untested statewide assessment system, instead opting to extend the current contract while allocating funds for a vendor to analyze options for overhauling the assessment system. **Last Bills on the Senate Floor** Eleven of my bills have passed the Senate and are headed to the House of Delegates. Five will be debated tomorrow on the Senate Floor. Wednesday we start presenting our bills in House Committees and hearing House bills in Senate Committees. There is less positive news regarding [SB982 Casino gaming; eligible host localities](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101503792/526183333/-1477983200?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmM5MTVlMTEtOTRlMi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5N2U5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=FWS1yuMmZYIXK5q2r4aMGd_i_iWxr-uX0yveJKNsASE=&emci=fe26d897-b8dc-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=bc915e11-94e2-ef11-88f8-0022482a97e9&ceid=22486556) \- the bill continues to move forward despite significant opposition in our community. Tomorrow, we will debate the bill. I have prepared arguments against it - including input from the recent [Fairfax County Board of Supervisors letter to the General Assembly](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101503794/526183336/1231446730?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmM5MTVlMTEtOTRlMi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5N2U5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=FWS1yuMmZYIXK5q2r4aMGd_i_iWxr-uX0yveJKNsASE=&emci=fe26d897-b8dc-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=bc915e11-94e2-ef11-88f8-0022482a97e9&ceid=22486556) and the [press release from the No Fairfax Casino coalition](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101503800/526183338/49945697?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmM5MTVlMTEtOTRlMi1lZjExLTg4ZjgtMDAyMjQ4MmE5N2U5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=FWS1yuMmZYIXK5q2r4aMGd_i_iWxr-uX0yveJKNsASE=&emci=fe26d897-b8dc-ef11-88f8-0022482a9579&emdi=bc915e11-94e2-ef11-88f8-0022482a97e9&ceid=22486556). We will continue to oppose the bill if it passes tomorrow and is presented in the House. Please know that I am following the frustrating, often cruel, actions of the federal Administration. The implications of their actions, particularly cutting federal funding of programs implemented by the states, and the statements around eliminating federal jobs are quite serious. I remain in close contact with our federal representatives. At the state level, we are working to ensure that Virginians have a safety net. Please know that my colleagues and I will stand up against proposed policies that would harm our residents and communities. Kindest regards,
---
---
Jennifer B. BoyskoMember, Senate of Virginia Representing the newly created 38th Senate District making up the northernmost portions of Fairfax County (Great Falls, Herndon, McLean, Reston, WolfTrap, parts of unincorporated Vienna and a slice of Falls Church) **Photo Gallery**
---
My office was happy to meet with multiple advocacy groups this week, including...
---
---
Brain Injury Association of Virginia, a statewide, primary source of information and personal support for individuals, families, and professionals whose lives have been impacted by brain injury.
---
---
ArtsFairfax, who expand support for and access to arts and culture opportunities for more than 1.2 million residents across Fairfax County, the City of Fairfax, and the City of Falls Church.
---
---
Families Forward Virginia - Connections Family Resource Center which work to ensure all families, youth and children are safe, healthy and nurtured, and have equitable access to resources and opportunities to thrive in their communities.
---
---
Future Farmers of America, the premier youth organization preparing members for leadership and careers in the science, business and technology of agriculture.
---
---
I was glad to introduce my three session interns, Olga Rokhlenko, Jason Sandler-Bussey and Ava Kegler from the Senate Floor.
---
---
With Representative Jennifer McClellan, speaking at the Electrifying Virginia press conference
---
---
On Wednesday, January 29th our office celebrated Chinese New Year. We hope all of you celebrating will enjoy a prosperous, peaceful and healthy year of the snake.
---
---
Good Shephard Church in Reston for Methodist Advocacy Day
---
---
Virginia Trial Lawyers are dedicated to promoting professionalism within the trial bar, enhancing the competency of trial lawyers, and protecting and preserving the liberties, rights, and benefits of an efficient and constitutionally sound judicial system
---
---
Virginia Museum Association
---
---
JCRC
---
---
---
I connected with constituents Jan and Justin who came to advocate for Brain Injury Services.
---
---
Children's Home Society of Virginia - advocating for adoption, post adoption, and foster care services in Virginia.
---
---
James Madison University Student Government Advocates
---
---
---
Meeting with SEIU32BJ and 512 Members including my constituents Yasmin and Javier.
---
---
Hearing from Fairfax County Clerk of Court Chris Falcon about the needs of the Virginia Court Clerks Association.
---
---
---
At the Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee meeting on Tuesday, I was happy to see many of my constituents from the No Fairfax Casino Coalition. I spoke against and voted against the bill which would allow a casino to come to Tysons Corner.
---
---
Hunter Mill School Board member Melanie Meren visited and updated us on issues affecting local schools.
---
---
One of my constituents, Radhika Murari, the owner of OmBaked in Reston, came to advocate for legislation as part of NORML's lobby day.
---
---
Louis and Sabrina from Amalgamated Transit Union Local 689 joined AFLCIO for their lobby day. We were happy to hear their concerns about transit funding and need for more union support. Thank you for operating the buses in Fairfax County!
---

The page for each bill lists the committee referral and there is a drop down menu that takes you to the committee page to figure out when the bill will be presented. We often get short notice. If you support one or more of my bills, email me at SenatorBoysko@senate.virginia.gov and we will add you to our stakeholder list and notify you when the bill(s) will be heard. Your support is very important to the success of my legislation.

**Unemployment Assistance and Resources for Job Searchers** For those eligible for benefits, the Virginia Employment Commission (VEC) requires that people receiving unemployment benefits show proof of job searches each week. clean_body: | | | Boysko Bulletin: Budget Reports Published As Crossover Nears Dear Peter, Sunday afternoon, I returned to Richmond early for a Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee meeting. After several weeks of reviewing budget amendments for legislation, requests for amendments unrelated to bills, and the Governor’s budget amendments, we released our . The House of Delegates also released their . As each Chamber moves forward this week to formally adopt its respective budget, the two versions will go to a conference committee made up of representatives from both bodies, who will work to develop a compromise budget. It is important to note that while the state has a significant surplus of over $2.5 billion in discretionary funding this year, the majority of those funds are non-recurring and cannot be used to meet ongoing needs. In contrast with the Governor’s proposed budget, the Senate’s focus is clear, to ensure that hardworking families keep more of the money they earn. The Senate budget rejects the Governor’s phased car tax rebates totaling $1.1 billion in favor of tax changes that will benefit more Virginians this year. These include one-time income tax rebates of $200 for single filers and $400 for joint filers, reducing tax liabilities by increasing the standard deduction amounts to $8,750/$17,500, and raising the state Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) to 20% of the federal level. The state EITC is a proven anti-poverty program. The Governor’s budget proposal would let this key tax credit for working families expire, which would raise taxes on these earners. We need state and federal tax policies that ensure hardworking people can keep more of what they earn. Corporations and the ultra-wealthy must pay their fair share toward funding the right priorities, such as housing, health care and child care. Corporations and wealthy CEOs hold too much power in this country. I’m here to fight for you, not the rich special interests that are keeping prices high. The Senate budget provides more than $400 million in additional direct aid increases for K-12 schools. This includes funding for big-ticket items, including fully lifting the support cap, creating a new state add-on for special education students, and providing the state share of $1,000 bonuses for school staff. While I would like to see ongoing salary increases, we did not have the recurring funds available this year. I fought for and am pleased that we fully lifted the support cap, which has reduced funding for critical support staff positions (instructional aides, cafeteria workers, custodians, etc.) in schools since the 2009 recession. In response to the recent JLARC study, we added funding for special education students. I will continue to oppose the Governor’s efforts to shift public education funding to private schools. The Senate removed the $50 million the Governor allocated for private and religious school vouchers. These types of programs have been shown to harm student outcomes for those who participate while also diverting critical resources away from public schools. We removed more than $60 million from the Governor’s budget for a new untested statewide assessment system, instead opting to extend the current contract while allocating funds for a vendor to analyze options for overhauling the assessment system. Last Bills on the Senate Floor Eleven of my bills have passed the Senate and are headed to the House of Delegates. Five will be debated tomorrow on the Senate Floor. Wednesday we start presenting our bills in House Committees and hearing House bills in Senate Committees. There is less positive news regarding \- the bill continues to move forward despite significant opposition in our community. Tomorrow, we will debate the bill. I have prepared arguments against it - including input from the recent and the . We will continue to oppose the bill if it passes tomorrow and is presented in the House. Please know that I am following the frustrating, often cruel, actions of the federal Administration. The implications of their actions, particularly cutting federal funding of programs implemented by the states, and the statements around eliminating federal jobs are quite serious. I remain in close contact with our federal representatives. At the state level, we are working to ensure that Virginians have a safety net. Please know that my colleagues and I will stand up against proposed policies that would harm our residents and communities. Kindest regards, Jennifer B. BoyskoMember, Senate of Virginia Representing the newly created 38th Senate District making up the northernmost portions of Fairfax County (Great Falls, Herndon, McLean, Reston, WolfTrap, parts of unincorporated Vienna and a slice of Falls Church) Photo Gallery My office was happy to meet with multiple advocacy groups this week, including... Brain Injury Association of Virginia, a statewide, primary source of information and personal support for individuals, families, and professionals whose lives have been impacted by brain injury. ArtsFairfax, who expand support for and access to arts and culture opportunities for more than 1.2 million residents across Fairfax County, the City of Fairfax, and the City of Falls Church. Families Forward Virginia - Connections Family Resource Center which work to ensure all families, youth and children are safe, healthy and nurtured, and have equitable access to resources and opportunities to thrive in their communities. Future Farmers of America, the premier youth organization preparing members for leadership and careers in the science, business and technology of agriculture. I was glad to introduce my three session interns, Olga Rokhlenko, Jason Sandler-Bussey and Ava Kegler from the Senate Floor. With Representative Jennifer McClellan, speaking at the Electrifying Virginia press conference On Wednesday, January 29th our office celebrated Chinese New Year. We hope all of you celebrating will enjoy a prosperous, peaceful and healthy year of the snake. Good Shephard Church in Reston for Methodist Advocacy Day Virginia Trial Lawyers are dedicated to promoting professionalism within the trial bar, enhancing the competency of trial lawyers, and protecting and preserving the liberties, rights, and benefits of an efficient and constitutionally sound judicial system Virginia Museum Association JCRC I connected with constituents Jan and Justin who came to advocate for Brain Injury Services. Children's Home Society of Virginia - advocating for adoption, post adoption, and foster care services in Virginia. James Madison University Student Government Advocates Meeting with SEIU32BJ and 512 Members including my constituents Yasmin and Javier. Hearing from Fairfax County Clerk of Court Chris Falcon about the needs of the Virginia Court Clerks Association. At the Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee meeting on Tuesday, I was happy to see many of my constituents from the No Fairfax Casino Coalition. I spoke against and voted against the bill which would allow a casino to come to Tysons Corner. Hunter Mill School Board member Melanie Meren visited and updated us on issues affecting local schools. One of my constituents, Radhika Murari, the owner of OmBaked in Reston, came to advocate for legislation as part of NORML's lobby day. Louis and Sabrina from Amalgamated Transit Union Local 689 joined AFLCIO for their lobby day. We were happy to hear their concerns about transit funding and need for more union support. Thank you for operating the buses in Fairfax County! Committee pages are where you will find when committees meet, their dockets, and what bills the committees are considering. The page for each bill lists the committee referral and there is a drop down menu that takes you to the committee page to figure out when the bill will be presented. We often get short notice. If you support one or more of my bills, email me at SenatorBoysko@senate.virginia.gov and we will add you to our stakeholder list and notify you when the bill(s) will be heard. Your support is very important to the success of my legislation. | | Constituent Services Addiction and Recovery Council As a member of the , we work together to ensure that people have the resources they need to identify and get help for addiction recovery.Addiction is a life threatening problem for too many Virginians. Please reach out if you need help for yourself or a loved one. Reach Out for Mental Health Support The is available nationwide. You can also find support for yourself and family members by reaching out to or. The Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration National Helpline: 1-800-662-4357 National Drug Helpline: 1-844-289-0879 National Institute of Mental Health Information Resource Center: 1-866-615-6464 Partnership for Drug-Free Kids: 1-855-378-4373 Unemployment Assistance and Resources for Job Searchers For those eligible for benefits, the Virginia Employment Commission (VEC) requires that people receiving unemployment benefits show proof of job searches each week. You can also reach out to your county - your local government will review your financial situation and connect you with resources. In Fairfax County, contact . are additional resources for food. If you have lost your health insurance, please visit to figure out if you are eligible for Medicaid or for help with Affordable Care Act insurance. Support for Our Immigrant Community Members I will continue to work to ensure that our communities are welcoming, diverse and provide opportunities for all people to live with dignity and safety. Working together, we can build good lives for our families and our communities. The news of ICE sweeps is distressing. We can help by sharing information with our neighbors regarding their rights. Please share this information from the ACLU, available in multiple languages: We are also working with the Legal Aid Justice Center which has asked that we share their . Our community is better when we embrace our diversity and keep each other safe! name: Oregon GOP
subject: A Time to Remember and Celebrate
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE OREGON REPUBLICAN PARTY (COMMITTEE ID 379) 187 HIGH ST
body:
Honoring Black History Month

| | | | [ ](https://oregon.gop/)
---
| | Friend, Last Friday, President Trump issued a proclamation declaring February 2025 Black History Month. We join him in honoring Black Americans who have helped shape our great nation. From leaders in government to pioneers in business, education, and the arts, Black Americans have played an essential role in advancing freedom, opportunity, and prosperity in the Republican Party and in our nation. The Republican Party has a long history of welcoming and being enriched by the contributions of black Americans, from America’s first black US Senator, Henry Rhodes Revel, to our very own Vice Chairwoman, Dr. Angela Plowhead. We celebrate the contributions Black Americans have made and continue to make to shape the landscape of America.
---
| | [ ](https://links.infooregongop.com/els/v2/j8dkhEpq4atv/dnByWjFGRGFQeHFuLzYzVkNkTkdxWmFsUFpoemhXeWhIdmxLRTQxeXVHZTBWSTJERlg5MllvT3NrY05TZ0xZeWx3ZmQ1U3ZKSUFaQ3FZeUhSK2xBV1JiRS9tUWNnUVh6K3BqSTlDdlVOMnc9S0/)
---
| | This month is an opportunity to reflect on the struggles and triumphs that have shaped our shared history. It is also a time to celebrate the values of perseverance, leadership, innovation, and resilience that continue to inspire Americans from all walks of life. By recognizing and celebrating the contributions of Black Americans, we honor the spirit of community that strengthens our nation and overcomes division. Together as one people and one nation we walk into this new Golden Age of America. Thank you, Oregon Republican Party
---

clean_body: I’m opening up a few more Grassroots Cabinet positions. Secure one of the few spots to advise me. | | | | | ANNOUNCEMENT FROM PRESIDENT TRUMP: Goodnight Friend! I’m opening up a few more OFFICIAL GRASSROOTS CABINET POSITIONS to celebrate the approval of several of my cabinet nominees. I’ve only extended this offer to a select group of my most loyal supporters. Since you’re reading this right now, that means YOU’RE ONE OF THEM! I need you to take one of my Trump Advisory Polls soon! To make this offer too good to pass up, I’m going to send you an official membership card when you accept. Sent from, President Trump | | | | | Contributions to Trump National Committee JFC Inc. are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. All contributions are subject to the limits and prohibitions of the Federal Election Campaign Act. name: Justice Democrats
subject: We are in a crisis, but we choose to fight
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

We are in a crisis, Peter.

Donald Trump, his far-right administration, and unelected billionaire Elon
Musk are undermining the federal government’s ability to function and
executing a hostile takeover as we speak.

First, the MAGA administration attempted to freeze TRILLIONS of dollars in
federal funding for government services and programs that help millions of
people get by.

Then, Musk and his aides are forcing out workers at the U.S. Agency for
International Development (USAID) and claiming the agency is being shut
down.

Now, Trump and Musk are trying to ram through the Senate confirmation of
Russell Vought, the nominee for Director of the Office of Management and
Budget (OMB), who is the human embodiment of the effort to freeze federal
funding and the architect of Project 2025.

This is a quiet corporate coup by Trump and the right-wing elite. Their
goal is clear: gut federal agencies, strip public resources, and use our
federal government to line their own pockets. But we won’t let it happen
without a fight.

A coalition led by Indivisible, Working Families Party, and MoveOn is
telling Trump, the GOP, and Democratic leadership: We Choose to Fight.

[ https://indivisible.org/resource/how-senate-democrats-can-shut-down-trumps-agenda-procedural-hardball?source=em20250203-36393 ]We’re mobilizing people across the country to urge their senators to do
everything in their power to delay, fight, and defeat Vought’s
confirmation. Learn how you can take action by organizing or attending a
U.S. Senate state office visit here →

[ https://indivisible.org/resource/how-senate-democrats-can-shut-down-trumps-agenda-procedural-hardball?source=em20250203-36393 ]TAKE ACTION

We cannot leave any procedural action, tactic, or stone unturned in the
fight against the Musk-Trump administration. We need our Democratic
leaders to use every tool available to stop this privatization of our
democracy.

Thank you for taking action in this critical moment for our country.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250203-36393-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36393_1203867_hHqvA7&akid=36393%2E1203867%2EhHqvA7

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36393%2E1203867%2EhHqvA7

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://support
body:
# [#]
Peter, my priority will always be to work for my constituents and fight for all Americans’ rights and freedoms.

I know these past few weeks of Republican leadership in the House, Senate, and the White House may have some people feeling defeated but we can’t stop now. I need to know my supporters are with me as we look ahead to 2026.

Our first monthly fundraising deadline of the year is only 72 hours away. We need 500 contributions to start this new year and launch a new campaign on the right foot. Will you please contribute $10 or anything you can today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mt-jan-25?amount=10]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately.

DONATE $10 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mt-jan-25?express_lane=true&amount=10] DONATE $25 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mt-jan-25?express_lane=true&amount=25]
DONATE $50 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mt-jan-25?express_lane=true&amount=50] DONATE $100 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mt-jan-25?express_lane=true&amount=100]
DONATE $200 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mt-jan-25?express_lane=true&amount=200] OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mt-jan-25?express_lane=true]

2026 is our next chance to take back the House. Even though 2026 seems far away, the work we do now will help us win.

In Congress, I will work across the aisle but I can’t ignore the GOP attempts to destroy sensible and responsible policy solutions that bolster the middle class, eliminate jobs, raise the cost of housing and health care, and endanger our communities.

Peter, I need your support. Please, chip in before Friday night to help us meet our goal of 500 contributions. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mt-jan-25?amount=10]

Thank you,

Mike Thompson
